title: Embelia viridiflora
taxonomy: Embelia viridiflora is classified under the family Primulaceae. This plant is native to the tropical regions of Asia, specifically found in India and Sri Lanka. It belongs to the genus Embelia, which is a group of flowering plants known for their medicinal properties. The classification of Embelia viridiflora is also reflected in its botanical name, which indicates its scientific identity. This classification helps in understanding the plant's characteristics and its place within the larger botanical system.
uses: Embelia viridiflora is a plant species that has been traditionally used in various parts of the world for its medicinal properties. Its roots, leaves, and fruits have been used in traditional medicine to treat various health conditions. The plant is said to possess anti-inflammatory and antioxidant properties, which may help in reducing pain and inflammation. In traditional medicine, Embelia viridiflora is used to treat digestive issues, fever, and respiratory problems. Further research is needed to confirm the efficacy and safety of using Embelia viridiflora for medicinal purposes.

side_effects: Embelia viridiflora is traditionally used in Ayurvedic medicine, and some studies suggest that its extracts may have various side effects. Embelia viridiflora extracts have been reported to have hepatoprotective effects, but excessive consumption may cause liver damage in some individuals. Furthermore, the extracts may interact with certain medications and worsen conditions like diabetes and high blood pressure. More research is needed to fully understand the potential side effects of Embelia viridiflora.
